headline: AMD And Samsung Partnership Adds New Piece To AI Supply Story
summary: AMD (NasdaqGS:AMD) and Samsung signed a new memorandum of understanding to supply next generation HBM4 memory for AMD Instinct AI GPUs. The agreement expands their existing partnership around advanced memory for AI data center hardware. The companies are also discussing potential foundry collaboration for future AMD products. For anyone tracking AI hardware, this move gives you another data point on how AMD is positioning itself in the accelerator race. The company already competes in GPUs,...

headline: AI Chips Today - AI Propels Satellite and Cellular Network Revolution
summary: Recent developments in AI-driven chip technology are noteworthy with Mavenir's recognition at the 2026 Satellite Mobile Innovation Awards. Mavenir was awarded the President's Award for Outstanding Innovation for its AI-driven, cloud-native solutions in both terrestrial and non-terrestrial networks. This innovation highlights the ongoing transformation in global connectivity, blending satellite and cellular technologies to provide widespread and resilient network coverage. The achievement...

headline: Nvidia Stock Rises. This New Customer Has Big Spending Plans.
summary: Nvidia  shares were gaining on Monday.  The chip maker is set to benefit from artificial-intelligence company Mistral AI’s plan to build a data center stuffed with its processors.  Nvidia was up 0.9% at $169.02 in premarket trading.

headline: AI Governance for Boards: A Framework by Robert Maciejko
summary: By Karen Roman A majority of institutions incorporate AI as part of their main business functions, yet many boards do not have formal oversight structures for AI governance, according to a governance framework paper published on research platform SSRN. The paper proposed a practical approach to support boards on AI management that includes the STAR […] The post AI Governance for Boards: A Framework by Robert Maciejko appeared first on CorpGov.

headline: AMD vs Intel: Which Stock Will Lead in 2026?
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) closed 2025 with record revenue and accelerating AI momentum, while Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) delivered a more complicated picture: a modest revenue beat, a GAAP net loss, and a foundry business still hemorrhaging cash. Both are chasing AI dominance in 2026, but from very different starting positions. We compare the tech giants to ... AMD vs Intel: Which Stock Will Lead in 2026?

headline: Intel Slides 5%, AMD Drops 3%: The Semiconductor Sector Is at a Crossroads
summary: Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) shares are sliding 5% in Monday trading, pulling back to the $41 area. Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) is also in the red, falling 3% to $196 and change. The pullback carries a sharp irony. Both stocks surged last Wednesday, March 25, after reports emerged that Intel and AMD plan to raise CPU prices ... Intel Slides 5%, AMD Drops 3%: The Semiconductor Sector Is at a Crossroads
